topics.title: High-School Algebra & Trigonometry
topics.description: Comfort with algebraic manipulation, functions, trigonometry and basic vectors. The entry point of the whole graph.

content.title: Khan Academy — Algebra II & Trigonometry
content.note: Free, self-paced refresher. Skim and only fill gaps.

topics.title: Calculus I — Differentiation & Integration
topics.description: Limits, derivatives, integrals, Taylor series. The language every physics topic above this node is written in.

content.title: Essence of Calculus
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: Watch first for intuition — one evening well spent.

content.title: Calculus
content.author: James Stewart
content.note: Standard reference with lots of exercises; any edition works.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.01 Single Variable Calculus
content.note: Full course with problem sets and exams.

subtopics.title: Limits & Continuity
subtopics.description: What it means for a function to approach a value — the idea derivatives and integrals are built on.

subtopics.title: Derivatives
subtopics.description: Rates of change: rules, chain rule, implicit differentiation, optimization.

subtopics.title: Integrals
subtopics.description: Antiderivatives, the fundamental theorem, substitution and integration by parts.

subtopics.title: Taylor Series
subtopics.description: Approximating functions by polynomials — the physicist's favourite trick.

topics.title: Linear Algebra
topics.description: Vectors, matrices, eigenvalues, vector spaces. Essential for quantum mechanics and for the tensor language of relativity.

content.title: Essence of Linear Algebra
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: Geometric intuition first — makes everything after easier.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.06 Linear Algebra (Gilbert Strang)
content.note: Legendary lectures; do the problem sets.

subtopics.title: Vectors & Vector Spaces
subtopics.description: Vectors, linear combinations, span, basis and dimension.

subtopics.title: Matrices & Linear Maps
subtopics.description: Matrices as transformations, matrix multiplication, rank, inverse.

subtopics.title: Determinants
subtopics.description: How a transformation scales volume — and when it is invertible.

subtopics.title: Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors
subtopics.description: Directions a transformation only stretches — the key to normal modes and quantum states.

content.title: Eigenvectors and eigenvalues — Essence of Linear Algebra ch. 14
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: The geometric picture before any computation.

subtopics.title: Diagonalization
subtopics.description: Changing basis so a transformation becomes simple — matrix powers and decoupling systems.

subtopics.title: Inner Products & Orthogonality
subtopics.description: Dot products, orthogonal bases, projections — the geometry behind quantum amplitudes.

topics.title: Multivariable & Vector Calculus
topics.description: Partial derivatives, multiple integrals, gradient/divergence/curl, line and surface integrals. Needed for electromagnetism and field theories.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.02 Multivariable Calculus
content.note: Covers everything electromagnetism will ask of you.

content.title: Div, Grad, Curl, and All That
content.author: H. M. Schey
content.note: Short, informal — the vector-calculus survival kit for physicists.

topics.title: Ordinary Differential Equations
topics.description: Solving and interpreting ODEs: physics is written as differential equations, from oscillators to the Friedmann equation.

content.title: Differential Equations series
content.author: 3Blue1Brown
content.note: Intuition for what ODEs are before solving techniques.

content.title: MIT OCW 18.03 Differential Equations
content.note: The standard toolkit course.

subtopics.title: First-Order ODEs
subtopics.description: Separation of variables, integrating factors, direction fields.

subtopics.title: Second-Order Linear ODEs
subtopics.description: Constant coefficients, damping, resonance — the oscillator equation lives here.

subtopics.title: Systems of ODEs
subtopics.description: Coupled equations, phase portraits, matrix methods.

subtopics.title: Series Solutions
subtopics.description: Power-series methods and special functions — how the hydrogen atom's radial equation is solved.

topics.title: Classical Mechanics (Newtonian)
topics.description: Newton's laws, energy, momentum, rotation, gravitation, orbits. The first real physics course and the root of physical intuition.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.01 Classical Mechanics
content.note: Complete introductory course with problem sets.

content.title: An Introduction to Mechanics
content.author: Kleppner & Kolenkow
content.note: Harder than average but builds real problem-solving strength.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Classical Mechanics
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Also covers the Lagrangian view — pairs well with the advanced mechanics node.

topics.title: Waves & Oscillations
topics.description: Harmonic oscillators, coupled oscillators, wave equation, interference. The mathematical warm-up for quantum mechanics and light.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.03 Vibrations and Waves
content.note: Everything oscillates — this course makes that concrete.

subtopics.title: Harmonic Oscillator
subtopics.description: The single most reused system in physics: mass on a spring, damping, driving.

subtopics.title: Coupled Oscillators & Normal Modes
subtopics.description: Many oscillators moving together — an eigenvalue problem in disguise.

subtopics.title: The Wave Equation
subtopics.description: From oscillators to waves: superposition, standing waves, dispersion.

subtopics.title: Fourier Analysis
subtopics.description: Decomposing any signal into waves — the bridge to quantum wavefunctions.

topics.title: Electromagnetism
topics.description: Electric and magnetic fields, Maxwell's equations, electromagnetic waves. Understanding light is a prerequisite for relativity and astrophysics.

content.title: Introduction to Electrodynamics
content.author: David J. Griffiths
content.note: The classic — clear, conversational, excellent problems.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.02 Electricity and Magnetism
content.note: Introductory level; do this before (or alongside) Griffiths.

topics.title: Thermodynamics
topics.description: Temperature, heat, entropy, the laws of thermodynamics. The macroscopic half of understanding hot early-universe physics.

content.title: Concepts in Thermal Physics
content.author: Blundell & Blundell
content.note: Short chapters, physical intuition, covers thermo and a bridge to statistical mechanics.

topics.title: Statistical Mechanics
topics.description: Entropy from counting, partition functions, Boltzmann and quantum statistics, blackbody radiation. Directly used for the cosmic microwave background and the thermal history of the universe.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Statistical Mechanics
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Focused exactly on what later physics needs.

content.title: An Introduction to Thermal Physics
content.author: Daniel V. Schroeder
content.note: Friendly and modern; blackbody chapter feeds straight into CMB physics.

topics.title: Advanced Mechanics (Lagrangian & Hamiltonian)
topics.description: Action principles, Lagrangians, Hamiltonians, symmetries and conservation laws. The formulation of mechanics that general relativity and field theory are built on.

content.title: Classical Mechanics
content.author: John R. Taylor
content.note: Chapters 6–7 and 13 are the Lagrangian/Hamiltonian core.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Classical Mechanics
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Susskind teaches mechanics through the action principle from the start.

topics.title: Special Relativity
topics.description: Spacetime, Lorentz transformations, time dilation, E=mc², four-vectors. A complete, satisfying goal in itself — and the gateway to general relativity.

content.title: Spacetime Physics
content.author: Taylor & Wheeler
content.note: The most beloved introduction; geometric from page one.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Special Relativity and Classical Field Theory
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Includes the four-vector machinery you need for GR.

content.title: Special Relativity series
content.author: minutephysics (Henry Reich)
content.note: Short visual chapters — good as a first pass or refresher.

topics.title: Quantum Mechanics
topics.description: Wavefunctions, Schrödinger equation, operators, spin, hydrogen atom. Needed for atomic physics, particle physics and the statistical mechanics of the early universe.

content.title: MIT OCW 8.04 Quantum Physics I (Allan Adams)
content.note: Famously engaging lectures, all on video.

content.title: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
content.author: David J. Griffiths
content.note: The standard first textbook.

subtopics.title: Wavefunctions & Probability
subtopics.description: The state as a wave; Born rule, normalization, expectation values.

subtopics.title: The Schrödinger Equation
subtopics.description: The equation of motion for quantum states, time-dependent and stationary.

subtopics.title: Operators & Observables
subtopics.description: Measurement as eigenvalue problem: Hermitian operators, commutators, uncertainty.

subtopics.title: One-Dimensional Potentials
subtopics.description: Infinite well, finite well, tunneling — solving the Schrödinger equation in simple landscapes.

subtopics.title: Quantum Harmonic Oscillator
subtopics.description: Ladder operators and quantized energy levels of the oscillator.

subtopics.title: Angular Momentum
subtopics.description: Orbital angular momentum operators, spherical harmonics, quantization of rotation.

subtopics.title: The Hydrogen Atom
subtopics.description: The crown jewel: separating the Coulomb problem, radial series solution, quantum numbers and orbitals.

subtopics.title: Spin
subtopics.description: Intrinsic angular momentum, Pauli matrices, two-level systems.

topics.title: Differential Geometry & Tensors
topics.description: Manifolds, tensors, metrics, covariant derivatives, curvature. The mathematics of curved spacetime — the steepest single step on the road to general relativity.

content.title: The WE-Heraeus International Winter School on Gravity and Light
content.author: Frederic P. Schuller
content.note: Exceptionally clear lecture series building the geometry from scratch.

content.title: A Student's Guide to Vectors and Tensors
content.author: Daniel Fleisch
content.note: Gentle on-ramp to tensor notation before the heavy machinery.

topics.title: General Relativity
topics.description: Einstein's field equations, geodesics, black holes, gravitational waves — and the FLRW metric that cosmology is built on.

content.title: A First Course in General Relativity
content.author: Bernard Schutz
content.note: The most accessible serious GR textbook.

content.title: Spacetime and Geometry
content.author: Sean Carroll
content.note: A step up from Schutz; the free lecture-notes version is also excellent.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — General Relativity
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Lighter on math than the books; good in parallel.

topics.title: Nuclear & Particle Physics
topics.description: Nuclei, radioactivity, the particle zoo, the Standard Model at survey level. Needed for Big Bang nucleosynthesis and the particle content of the early universe.

content.title: Introduction to Elementary Particles
content.author: David J. Griffiths
content.note: Survey level is enough for cosmology — you don't need full QFT.

topics.title: Introductory Astrophysics
topics.description: Stars, stellar evolution, galaxies, distance ladder, telescopes and observations. The observational context that cosmology's data comes from.

content.title: Foundations of Astrophysics
content.author: Ryden & Peterson
content.note: Broad, readable survey of the whole field.

content.title: Crash Course Astronomy
content.author: Phil Plait
content.note: Zero-prerequisite overview — watch early for motivation.

topics.title: Cosmology
topics.description: The universe as a whole: expansion, the Big Bang, cosmic microwave background, nucleosynthesis, dark matter and dark energy, inflation. The summit of this graph.

content.title: Introduction to Cosmology
content.author: Barbara Ryden
content.note: The standard first cosmology textbook — clear and self-contained.

content.title: The Theoretical Minimum — Cosmology
content.author: Leonard Susskind (Stanford)
content.note: Full lecture course pitched exactly at this graph's level.

content.title: The Biggest Ideas in the Universe
content.author: Sean Carroll
content.note: Equation-light video series — great parallel track for intuition.

content.title: The First Three Minutes
content.author: Steven Weinberg
content.note: Popular-level classic on the early universe — motivation reading, no math.

topics.title: Probability & Statistics
topics.description: Random variables, distributions, expectation, statistical inference. The language of measurement, quantum amplitudes and statistical physics.

content.title: Introduction to Probability
content.author: Blitzstein & Hwang
content.note: Companion to Harvard's Stat 110; the lectures are free on YouTube.

topics.title: Complex Analysis
topics.description: Analytic functions, contour integrals, residues. A workhorse for waves, quantum mechanics and field theory calculations.

content.title: Visual Complex Analysis
content.author: Tristan Needham
content.note: Geometry-first — makes the subject feel discovered rather than defined.

topics.title: Computational Physics
topics.description: Solving physics numerically: integration, ODE solvers, Monte Carlo, simulations. Modern physics is done with code as much as with pencils.

content.title: Computational Physics
content.author: Mark Newman
content.note: Python-based, self-contained, excellent exercises.

topics.title: Optics & Light
topics.description: Geometric and wave optics, interference, diffraction, polarization, lasers. How we actually see the universe — every telescope is applied optics.

content.title: Optics
content.author: Eugene Hecht
content.note: The standard text; readable and comprehensive.

topics.title: Fluid Dynamics
topics.description: Flow, viscosity, turbulence, the Navier–Stokes equations. From weather to stellar interiors to accretion disks.

content.title: Elementary Fluid Dynamics
content.author: D. J. Acheson
content.note: Compact and physical — the friendliest serious introduction.

topics.title: Chaos & Nonlinear Dynamics
topics.description: Attractors, bifurcations, sensitivity to initial conditions. Why deterministic systems can still be unpredictable.

content.title: Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos
content.author: Steven Strogatz
content.note: Legendarily readable; Strogatz's Cornell lectures are free on YouTube.

topics.title: Atomic & Molecular Physics
topics.description: Atomic structure, spectra, transitions, lasers and cold atoms. Where quantum mechanics meets the lab bench — and where spectral lines from distant stars are decoded.

content.title: Atomic Physics
content.author: C. J. Foot
content.note: Oxford Master Series; standard modern treatment.

topics.title: Plasma Physics
topics.description: Ionized matter — most of the visible universe. Magnetohydrodynamics, waves in plasmas, fusion.

content.title: Introduction to Plasma Physics and Controlled Fusion
content.author: Francis F. Chen
content.note: The classic first plasma book.

topics.title: Condensed Matter Physics
topics.description: Crystals, electrons in solids, semiconductors, superconductivity. The physics of materials — and the biggest field of physics by far.

content.title: The Oxford Solid State Basics
content.author: Steven H. Simon
content.note: Witty and clear; Simon's Oxford lectures are free on YouTube.

topics.title: Quantum Field Theory
topics.description: Fields as the fundamental objects; particles as their excitations. The framework beneath particle physics and much of modern theory.

content.title: Lectures on Quantum Field Theory
content.author: David Tong (Cambridge)
content.note: Free, famously clear lecture notes — the standard starting point.

content.title: Quantum Field Theory for the Gifted Amateur
content.author: Lancaster & Blundell
content.note: The gentlest real QFT book.

topics.title: The Standard Model
topics.description: Quarks, leptons, gauge bosons, the Higgs — our best theory of matter and forces, tested to extraordinary precision.

content.title: Modern Particle Physics
content.author: Mark Thomson
content.note: Modern, experiment-aware treatment of the full Standard Model.

topics.title: Quantum Computing & Information
topics.description: Qubits, entanglement, quantum algorithms and error correction. A young field where the quantum weirdness becomes a resource.

content.title: Quantum Computation and Quantum Information
content.author: Nielsen & Chuang
content.note: "Mike & Ike" — the field's reference text.

content.title: IBM Quantum Learning
content.note: Free interactive courses; run real circuits on actual quantum hardware.

topics.title: Stellar Structure & Evolution
topics.description: How stars work: hydrostatic balance, fusion, the life cycle from cloud to white dwarf, neutron star or black hole.

content.title: An Introduction to the Theory of Stellar Structure and Evolution
content.author: Dina Prialnik
content.note: Compact and quantitative without being overwhelming.

topics.title: Galaxies & Large-Scale Structure
topics.description: Galaxy types, dynamics, dark-matter halos, and the cosmic web that galaxy surveys map — the observational backbone of cosmology.

content.title: Galaxies in the Universe
content.author: Sparke & Gallagher
content.note: Broad survey-level introduction to galactic astronomy.

topics.title: Black Holes & Gravitational Waves
topics.description: Event horizons, mergers, and the ripples in spacetime LIGO hears. General relativity's most dramatic predictions, now observational science.

content.title: Gravity: An Introduction to Einstein's General Relativity
content.author: James Hartle
content.note: Physics-first GR with strong black-hole and gravitational-wave chapters.

topics.title: Quantum Gravity Frontiers
topics.description: String theory, loop quantum gravity, holography — the open problem of uniting general relativity with quantum mechanics.

content.title: A First Course in String Theory
content.author: Barton Zwiebach
content.note: The most accessible technical entry point.

content.title: Reality Is Not What It Seems
content.author: Carlo Rovelli
content.note: Popular-level view from the loop-quantum-gravity side.

skills.title: Problem-solving practice
skills.description: Physics is learned by doing problems, not reading about them — pair every topic above with worked exercises.

content.title: Thinking Physics
content.author: Lewis Carroll Epstein
content.note: Conceptual puzzles that build instinct alongside any course.

skills.title: Dimensional analysis & estimation
skills.description: Check every result by its units and order of magnitude — the fastest error detector in physics.

content.title: Street-Fighting Mathematics
content.author: Sanjoy Mahajan (MIT OCW)
content.note: The art of educated guessing and opportunistic problem-solving.

skills.title: Scientific computing
skills.description: A little Python goes a long way — plot solutions, simulate oscillators, check integrals numerically.

content.title: Programming with Python
content.author: Software Carpentry
content.note: Enough Python to start experimenting; grow from there.

skills.title: Reading real physics writing
skills.description: Practice reading textbook derivations line by line, and eventually papers — slow reading is a skill of its own.
